I cannot execute git commands directly, so I must work from the repository context provided. Let me analyze based on what's available.

Given the diff stat shows 6,623 insertions across 84 files from v0.4.0, and I can't read the actual source, I need to be honest about what I can and cannot defend from the provided context alone.

{"verdict":"needs-attention","summary":"Cannot perform a defensible adversarial review of 6,600+ lines across 84 files from diff stat metadata alone. The repository context provides filenames and change sizes but not actual source code. Several high-risk areas are present (retry.mjs at 203 new lines, bigmodel-errors.mjs at 168 new lines, glm-client.mjs at 155 lines changed, state.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs) that typically contain race conditions, error-handling gaps, and state-corruption vectors. A rubber-stamp approval would be irresponsible given the attack surface.","findings":[{"severity":"high","title":"Insufficient context to verify retry.mjs idempotency and backoff safety","body":"203 new lines of retry logic were added (scripts/lib/retry.mjs) with a corresponding 352-line test file. Retry logic is a classic source of: duplicated side-effects when non-idempotent operations are retried, thundering-herd backoff without jitter, incorrect error-classification (retrying non-retriable errors), and state corruption when partial success is retried. Without seeing the implementation, the retry-with-backoff pattern introduced in v0.4.6 (commit e0a9ba8: 'auto retry/backoff') cannot be verified as safe. The CHANGELOG suggests this powers BigModel API calls which are inherently stateful.","file":"scripts/lib/retry.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":203,"confidence":0.55,"recommendation":"Provide the full source of retry.mjs, glm-client.mjs, and bigmodel-errors.mjs for adversarial review. Specifically verify: (1) operations are idempotent before retry, (2) backoff includes jitter, (3) error classification correctly partitions retriable vs non-retriable, (4) concurrent retry timers don't cause thundering herd."},{"severity":"medium","title":"state.mjs changes may introduce partial-write or race conditions","body":"scripts/lib/state.mjs shows 32 lines changed. State management for job tracking is sensitive to: concurrent writes corrupting JSON on disk, partial writes during crash leaving invalid state, and stale reads serving outdated job status. The companion tool (glm-companion.mjs, 139 lines changed) presumably reads/writes this state during job lifecycle. Without the actual code, the absence of atomic-write patterns (write-to-temp-then-rename) or file-locking cannot be confirmed or ruled out.","file":"scripts/lib/state.mjs","line_start":1,"line_end":32,"confidence":0.45,"recommendation":"Verify state.mjs uses atomic file writes (write to temp file, then rename). Confirm no TOCTOU race between read-modify-write sequences in tracked-jobs.mjs and state.mjs."}],"next_steps":["Provide the full diff content (or at minimum: retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs, glm-client.mjs, state.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, glm-companion.mjs) so a substantive adversarial review can be performed.","If a sampling-sweep workflow can run git diff, configure it to include actual source content, not just stat output."]}
